title: oxalis rupestris
intro: Oxalis rupestris, also known as wood-sorrel or mountain wood-sorrel, is a low-growing, perennial plant species in the oxalis family, characterized by its small, white or pink flowers and heart-shaped leaves that resemble clover. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as a diuretic, to reduce inflammation, and to treat digestive issues. The health benefits of oxalis rupestris include its potential to reduce blood pressure, alleviate symptoms of arthritis, and provide antioxidant properties. Oxalis rupestris can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in herbal remedies and supplements, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and potential side effects.
